If it's multi-use, it shouldn't fold to `x` because the other uses of `fabs(x)` would get `x`, which is negative, instead of `fabs(x)` which is positive. So passing `NSZ=false` is correct. What if `x` is known positive? `fneg(fabs(x))` -> `fneg(x)`. This is handled by `KnownSrc.SignBit == false` which returns `replaceOperand(*I, 0, FNegFAbsSrc)` in the single-use case. Wait, `simplifyDemandedFPClassFnegFabs` returns `Src` if `KnownSrc.SignBit == true`. If `KnownSrc.SignBit == true`, `Src` is known negative. Then `fabs(Src)` is `-Src`. `fneg(fabs(Src))` is `Src`.
